verbatim_text: Yeah. Yeah, yeah. I agree that we are, in some sense, our interpretability tools are limited in how well we can actually run them and how well they can actually describe the underlying phenomenon. I think basically my sense is, as a first stab, probably the best thing we can do is match these interpretability tools, but we can do this in a much more scalable way. But secondly, if we enable just this general purpose AI system to also have this introspective mechanism, to also be able to utilize these interpretables, then theoretically they can also compose these interpretables either through chain of thought or internally or compose them with their existing reasoning skills. And we might be able to get to a future where we do also beat these interpretables. Okay, I think that's time to speak up.
